name: Guillaume shopping for Lisa
description: Partner + mother of their son, Mother's Day, design/yoga person
opener: I need a gift for Lisa, my partner and mother of our son. Mother's Day.

followUps.interests: She's really into design, reading, yoga and pilates. Very intentional about what she brings into the house.
followUps.direction: The wellness one feels right — something for her yoga practice
followUps.expression: I want her to feel appreciated as both my partner and an amazing mom
followUps.past_gifts: I got her a nice candle set last year, she liked it but it wasn't super memorable

name: Maria shopping for her dad
description: Close relationship, retirement party, practical outdoorsy dad
opener: My dad is retiring next month and I want to get him something really special.

followUps.interests: He's been talking about finally having time for fishing and woodworking. He's pretty practical — hates useless stuff.
followUps.budget: I'd go up to $300 for this
followUps.direction: Something for his woodworking — he keeps using the same old tools
followUps.expression: I want him to know I'm proud of his career and excited for his next chapter
followUps.past_gifts: Last birthday I got him a book about fly fishing and he actually read the whole thing

name: Alex shopping for best friend Sam
description: Close friend, birthday, user has no idea what to get — diffuse intent
opener: My best friend Sam's birthday is in 2 weeks and I have absolutely no idea what to get.

followUps.interests: He's a software engineer, plays guitar on weekends, loves craft beer. Pretty minimalist though — his apartment is super clean.
followUps.not_sure: I really don't know what direction
followUps.direction: The guitar one — he's always playing
followUps.expression: Just want him to know he's my best friend, you know?
followUps.past_gifts: I've never actually gotten him a great gift, that's why I'm stressing

name: Jordan thanking their boss
description: Professional relationship, thank you gift, tricky power dynamic
opener: I want to get a thank you gift for my boss. She really went to bat for my promotion.

followUps.interests: She's really into cooking and hosts dinner parties. She's also a big reader — always recommending books in our 1:1s.
followUps.not_sure: I don't want it to seem like I'm sucking up
followUps.budget: Maybe $50-75? I don't want it to be too much
followUps.direction: Something cooking related but elevated — not generic
followUps.past_gifts: The team got her flowers once but that felt impersonal

name: Priya shopping for sister's wedding
description: Very close sibling, wedding, high emotional stakes
opener: My sister Ananya is getting married in June. I'm the maid of honor and I want to give her something personal, not just off the registry.

followUps.interests: She's a photographer, loves travel, and she's really sentimental — she kept every birthday card I ever wrote her.
followUps.not_sure: I want something that makes her cry happy tears, you know?
followUps.direction: Something sentimental that captures our relationship
followUps.expression: That she's my person and I'm so happy for her
followUps.past_gifts: For her 30th I made a photo album of our childhood and she still has it on her coffee table

name: Chris bringing a housewarming gift
description: Casual acquaintance, low info, just needs something appropriate
opener: I'm going to a housewarming party this weekend for a coworker. Don't know them super well.

followUps.interests: They seem into cooking? They always bring homemade stuff to the office.
followUps.direction: Whatever is safe and appropriate — I just don't want to show up empty handed
followUps.past_gifts: no idea, first time getting them anything

name: Uncle Dave shopping for teenage niece
description: Extended family, holiday gift, generational gap
opener: Need a Christmas gift for my 16-year-old niece. I'm the fun uncle but I have no idea what teenagers are into.

followUps.interests: She's really into art — drawing, painting. She posts her stuff on social media. She's also into K-pop but I don't know anything about that.
followUps.not_sure: I don't want to get something cringey
followUps.expression: That I see her as creative and cool, not just a kid
followUps.past_gifts: Last year I got her a gift card and felt lame about it

name: ADHD user — anxious about anniversary gift
description: Classic ADHD target user — paralyzed by options, time blind, high anxiety
opener: oh god our anniversary is in 3 days and i completely forgot. my wife is going to kill me. help

followUps.interests: She likes gardening and baking and true crime podcasts. We've been together 8 years.
followUps.not_sure: i don't know i just need something fast that doesn't look last minute
followUps.budget: whatever, $100-150? just needs to arrive in time
followUps.direction: something that feels personal not like I panic-bought on amazon
followUps.expression: that I love her even though I'm a disaster at remembering dates
followUps.past_gifts: honestly she's told me before she wishes I'd put more thought into gifts instead of just getting something expensive
